items.title: Klopp's job at risk? 'Nonsense,' says Red Bull
items.summary: Red Bull backed former Liverpool manager Jürgen Klopp to remain the drinks giant's head of global soccer and dismissed a media report that his job could be under threat after barely a year.

items.title: Liverpool's Slot backs Salah to end PL drought
items.summary: Arne Slot has backed Liverpool forward Mohamed Salah to rediscover his goalscoring form, insisting "he always scores in the end."

items.title: Neymar shushes critics after first goals in return
items.summary: Neymar broke his 2026 goal drought in spectacular fashion Thursday, leading Santos to a 2-1 victory over Vasco da Gama in the fourth round of the Brasileirão.

items.title: Achtste finale Conference League: alle clubs en mogelijke affiches
items.summary: We zijn aangekomen in de achtste finale van de Conference League. Daarin zijn nog zestien clubs vertegenwoordigd, waaronder AZ, dat donderdag het Armeense FC Noah uitschakelde. Voetbal International schetst welke clubs door zijn én wat de affiches in de volgende ronde kunnen worden.

Neymar krijgt het aan de stok met oude bekende: 'Hij is een idioot'
items.summary: Neymar heeft het in de nacht van donderdag op vrijdag aan de stok gekregen met Thiago Mendes. De twee hadden al een vervelend verleden in Frankrijk en botsten in Brazilië wederom.

items.title: Liverpool’s title win last season fuelled by Premier League’s highest wage bill
items.summary: Wage bill increased by £42m to £428m, accounts show Revenue hit record £703m but profit was modest £8m Liverpool had the highest wage bill in the Premier League when winning their 20th league title last season, the club’s latest set of accounts have revealed. Liverpool’s wage bill increased by £42m to £428m in the year ending 31 May 2025, when a Premier League title triumph in Arne Slot’s debut season as head coach and a return to the Champions League increased revenue to a record £703m. The club’s wages-to-revenue ratio stood at a healthy 61%. It was the biggest wage bill in the division, ahead of Manchester City on £408m. Continue reading...

items.title: Topscorer bij VVV? 'Er worden in de kleedkamer al geintjes over gemaakt'
items.summary: Dean Zandbergen (24) was jarenlang op de amateurvelden in de Randstad actief, voordat hij op zijn 22ste zijn eerste profcontract tekende bij Dordrecht. Nu is hij iets meer dan een jaar spits bij VVV-Venlo. In de derde periode is hij basisspeler bij de Limburgers en met zes treffers aast hij stiekem op de topscorerstitel in de periode.

items.title: Slot wijst naar Gakpo na kritiek op Salah: 'Cody scoort ook niet zo vaak meer'
items.summary: Arne Slot heeft goede hoop dat Mohamed Salah snel de oude is. De Egyptenaar kent een moeizaam seizoen, maar zijn manager denkt dat de vleugelspits op korte termijn zijn stempel weer zal drukken. Daar is de hoop in Liverpool ook op.

items.title: So much for three-year plan - what is the secret to manager longevity?
items.summary: In his BBC Sport column, ex-Premier League boss Tony Pulis explains why it is so hard for football managers to keep their jobs in the modern game.

items.title: Bondscoach Portugal: 'Ronaldo helemaal niet bezig met duizendste doelpunt'
items.summary: Roberto Martínez ziet een mentaliteitsverandering bij Cristiano Ronaldo. Waar de Portugese vedette voorheen gefixeerd was op het maken van doelpunten, is de 41-jarige aanvaller volgens de bondscoach van Portugal meer en meer een teamspeler geworden. Dat bleek volgens Martínez al tijdens het EK. De Spaanse trainer stelt dan ook dat het maken van zijn duizendste goal geen doel op zich is voor Ronaldo, terwijl dit juist een belangrijke bron van motivatie leek voor de sterspeler van Al-Nassr om zo lang door te gaan.

items.title: Ruben Amorim sacking will cost Manchester United up to £15.9m
items.summary: Cost of Ten Hag exit then having Amorim may hit £36.3m Settlement for Amorim and his staff revealed in filing Manchester United sacking Ruben Amorim could end up costing the club almost £16m. Amorim’s 14-month reign ended on 5 January after his public attack on United’s hierarchy, with his five coaches also leaving Old Trafford. A filing to the New York Stock Exchange revealed the potential payments to Amorim and his staff, a day after the club confirmed they had made a £32.6m profit in their second-quarter results to 31 December 2025. Continue reading...

items.title: Rhodes stays in hunt, two off lead at Women's World Championship
items.summary: England's Mimi Rhodes remains two shots off leader Auston Kim after the second round of the HSBC Women's World Championship in Singapore.

items.title: Will Aston Villa hold on to their place in the Champions League?
items.summary: Villa’s run of three wins in nine league games has opened the door to Manchester United, Chelsea and Liverpool By WhoScored Two months ago, Aston Villa were just three points off top spot in the Premier League. They were on a run of 12 wins in 14 games that included victories against Manchester City and Arsenal. Their run of eight consecutive wins in the league was their best since they won 10 in a row in 1910. You wouldn’t have blamed Villa fans for daring to dream about lifting their first league title since 1981. But success breeds expectation, and expectation carries its own cruel weight. Arsenal tore Villa apart in a 4-1 win in late December and victories have been hard to come by in the new year – they have only won three of their last nine league games. Liverpool, Manchester United and Chelsea are still below them in the table but the gap is narrowing. With games against United and Chelsea coming in March, the top-three spot Villa have occupied since they beat Wolves in late November suddenly looks in jeopardy. So does their place in the Champions League next season. Continue reading...

items.title: Warren: Dubois' pre-fight party 'can't happen again'
items.summary: The pre-fight ‘party’ that took place ahead of Daniel Dubois’ defeat to Oleksandr Usyk “can’t happen again”, says promoter Frank Warren.
